title: Find Anyone
objective: In a 3-minute run, move a bottom-screen finder tray left and right to catch only the currently requested 'Anyone' tokens falling from a towering game library while avoiding social icons, empty boxes, and mismatched people; build combos for

controls.input: Drag left or right with one thumb anywhere on the lower half of the screen
controls.outcome: The tray glides under falling targets with mobile-friendly, one-hand control

controls.input: Keep catching correct Anyone tokens in a row
controls.outcome: Your combo and score multiplier increase

controls.input: Let a target fall, catch the wrong Anyone, or collect a social decoy
controls.outcome: You lose one focus heart and your combo resets

steps: A search prompt at the top says who to find right now, such as 'Find Anyone with glasses' or 'Find Anyone holding a controller'.
Tiny Anyone characters and decoys rain down in mixed lanes from the library shelves above.
Catch matching Anyone tokens in the tray to score and raise a combo multiplier.
Avoid social-feed decoys like notification bubbles, autoplay arrows, and empty game cases; each mistake costs a focus heart.
Every few seconds, a 'doomscroll surge' flips the search rule for 4 seconds: the tray magnetizes decoys, so you must briefly dodge almost it
difficulty ? Wait invalid. Need valid string. Let's rewrite complete JSON.
scoring: Each correct catch scores 10 points. Every 5 correct catches in a row adds +1 multiplier up to x5. During a doomscroll surge, surviving the full flip window grants a 'Found My Focus' bonus worth 50 points and preserves a
tips: Stay near center so your thumb can reach either side quickly.
Listen for the soft chime on correct catches and the scratchy buzz that signals an incoming doomscroll surge.
Sometimes skipping a risky catch is better than losing a heart and your multiplier.
Because the game stores best scores locally first, it works offline and can sync a simple leaderboard when a connection returns.

trendContext.title: Anyone find themselves opening up their game libraries, staring at it for a couple of hours, then just giving up and going on social media?
